title: What are Numerical Methods?
english: Numerical methods are mathematical tools used to find numerical approximations to the solutions of problems. Unlike analytical methods that give exact answers (like symbolic integration), numerical methods use arithmetic operations to get a result that is 'close enough' for practical purposes.
taglish: "So basically, ang Numerical Methods ay mga tools para makakuha ng sagot sa math problems gamit ang numbers. Unlike sa Analytical methods na exact ang sagot (yung tipong may x and y pa), dito estimation lang pero sobrang lapit na sa totoo. Gamit na gamit ito kapag sobrang hirap i-solve ng equation manually."

title: Why do we need them?
english: Many real-world mathematical problems are impossible or too difficult to solve analytically. Numerical methods allow computers to solve complex equations, integrals, and differential equations that humans cannot solve by hand.
taglish: "Kasi sa totoo lang, maraming math problems sa real world ang hindi kayang solusyunan ng pen and paper lang. Dito pumapasok ang computers. Since hindi marunong mag-algebra ang computer sa parehong paraan ng tao, numerical methods ang gamit nila para i-crunch ang numbers at bigyan tayo ng sagot."

title: Precision vs. Accuracy
english: Accuracy refers to how close a measured value is to the true value. Precision refers to how close the measurements are to each other, regardless of whether they are correct.
taglish: "Imagine mo naglalaro ka ng darts. Ang Accuracy ay kung gaano kalapit yung tama mo sa bullseye. Ang Precision naman, kung paulit-ulit kang tumira, magkakatabi ba yung mga tama mo? Pwedeng precise ka (magkakatabi) pero di accurate (malayo sa bullseye)."

title: Significant Figures
english: Significant figures are the digits in a number that contribute to its precision. They include all non-zero digits, zeros between non-zero digits, and trailing zeros in a decimal number.
taglish: "Eto yung mga numero na 'may kwenta' o importante. Hindi lahat ng zero ay significant. Mahalaga ito kasi dito natin malalaman kung gaano ka-precise ang computation natin. Kapag sinabing 3 significant figures, hanggang tatlong numero lang ang sure tayo na tama."

title: Round-off Errors
english: Round-off errors occur because computers have a limited number of bits to represent numbers. Some numbers, like 1/3 or Pi, cannot be represented exactly and must be rounded, leading to small errors.
taglish: "Nangyayari ito kasi hindi unli ang memory ng computer. Halimbawa, yung 1/3 diba 0.33333... forever? Since kailangan putulin ng computer yung decimal sa dulo, nagkakaroon ng maliit na error. Yun ang round-off error. Maliit lang pero pwedeng lumaki pag paulit-ulit."

title: Truncation Errors
english: Truncation errors happen when an infinite process is replaced by a finite one. For example, using only the first few terms of a Taylor Series to approximate a function creates a truncation error.
taglish: "Ito naman yung error dahil 'chinop' o pinutol mo yung process. Kunwari may formula na infinite ang haba, pero ginamit mo lang yung first 3 terms para mapabilis. Yung difference nung result mo sa result kung ginamit mo yung buo, yun ang Truncation Error."

title: Absolute vs. Relative Error
english: Absolute error is the simple difference between the true value and the approximate value. Relative error is the absolute error divided by the true value, often expressed as a percentage to show the error's scale.
taglish: "Ang Absolute Error ay kung gaano kalayo ang sagot mo sa tama (Subtraction lang). Ang Relative Error naman, icocompare mo yung layo na yun sa laki ng true value. Mas magandang gamitin ang Relative Error para makita kung malala ba talaga yung pagkamali mo o hindi."

title: Iterative Methods
english: Iterative methods are mathematical procedures that generate a sequence of improving approximate solutions for a class of problems. You start with a guess and keep refining it until the error is negligible.
taglish: "Parang 'trial and error' pero matalino. Magsisimula ka sa hula, tapos may formula ka para pagandahin yung hula mo. Uulit-ulitin mo lang hanggang sa sobrang lapit na nung sagot sa hinahanap mo. Yun ang iteration."

title: Convergence
english: Convergence is the property of an iterative method to approach the true solution as the number of iterations increases. If a method does not approach the solution, it is said to diverge.
taglish: "Ang Convergence ay yung unti-unting paglapit ng sagot mo sa katotohanan habang umuulit ka. Pag sinabing Divergence, palayo ng palayo ang sagot mo habang tumatagal—parang relasyon na walang patutunguhan. Gusto natin nagco-converge."

title: Applications of Numerical Methods
english: Numerical methods are essential in engineering simulations, weather forecasting, optimization problems, and computer graphics—anywhere analytical solutions are impossible.
taglish: "Saan to ginagamit? Sa pag-predict ng weather, pag-design ng bridges at buildings, at kahit sa video games physics. Lahat ng bagay na sobrang complex na di kaya ng simpleng math, numerical methods ang bumubuhat."
